item.title: Today, several new laws come into force in Norway. One of these is a duty to label retouched advertising.
item.description:
NOW RETAILED ADVERTISEMENT MUST BE MARKED From today, advertisements and sponsored posts must be marked if they contain people who have had their skin or body shape changed. The mark itself is a visible white circle with the text «retouched person».
WANTS TO AVOID BODY PRESSURE According to Ministry of Children and Families Kjersti Toppe (Sp), the purpose of the new requirement is to counteract body pressure and unnatural body ideals. - You should be aware of what is not natural, Toppe told VG earlier in June.
CLEAR FEEDBACK FROM YOUNG PEOPLE Norway is one of the first countries in the world to introduce a requirement for the labeling of retouched advertising. According to the Minister, the new rule should actually have several exceptions. But thanks to clear feedback from young people in the consultation round, the exceptions were scrapped.
STRICTER REQUIREMENTS FOR COSMETIC INTERVENTIONS Fighting body pressure is the purpose of another new decision. Competence requirements have now been introduced to carry out certain forms of cosmetic procedures, injections and treatment. An age limit of 18 years has also been introduced for such non-medical interventions. Source: https://www.vg.no/nyheter/innenriks/i/g6oP50/dette-skjer-i-dag?fbclid=IwAR30SGK81MWSnEYfCNvoA-8OlnIPp3mo8xLJMTrPFA7IKEDz4wCJRqVqb4Y
item.dc:creator: NaV3P

item.title: Ah yes - high school
item.description: Same goes for any student who dreams of a college athletic scholarship. The coach decides who plays and who warms the bench. Don't play and the scouts don't see you. Goodbye scholarship.

item.title: The Channel Tunnel is a 50.46-kilometre railway tunnel that connects Folkestone with Coquelles beneath the English Channel at the Strait of Dover. It is the only fixed link between the island of Great Britain and the European mainland.
item.dc:creator: Joyika

item.title: A wholesome dog dump just for you gals and guys
item.description:
Bernie wants it. I don't blame him
High 0.5
These dudes are gonna all get married soon with that level of heart
My US friends can probably relate this weekend
I'm trying to help my friend pull his little lady out of some serious kidney issues stemming from a poisoning she went through a couple years ago. The fight has been long and hard but we still hope they can get out of them in the end. If you can help them out, please do! https://gofund.me/d8e3dd17 And we're over half! you guys are amazing!
item.dc:creator: thedeadredhead

item.title: Pyro dump
item.description: July is here
item.dc:creator: NishtarianDrag

item.title: It's a long road, but it does lead somewhere good
item.description: I just wanted to share this story because I know a lot of people are going through something similar. In 2014 I was hit with a pretty major depression caused by burnout after having to work 2-3 jobs at a time while taking care of a sick family member for a long time. I don't really remember much about 2015 or 2016 other than going to a lot of different psychiatrists and being on a lot of different meds. I gained 70 pounds in 6 months. In 2017 I was finally well enough to start doing small things. So I started learning about psychology and mental health. The subject fascinated me so I kept studying. In 2019 I was able to enter the workplace again. I was lucky enough to have a family member who had his own company, where I was able to start with as low as a couple of hours a week and increase it at my own speed. Last year I was doing full time work again and I've spend the last 11 months or so trying to handle the stress of full time work again. Last month, I had finally gotten used to it enough to feel like I had energy left after work and I started doing projects again. I've had projects in the last 8 years but they were always with a slight agenda. Either therapuetic or to try to help our poor economic situation or simething like that, but now I'm doing things simply because I enjoy them. Since 2020 I've had the urge to tell people about the things I've learned that helped me to slowly get better and I've finally managed to start a YT channel and create the first few videos. I've always had a strong drive to help other people (which was a part of the reason why I burned out in the first place) so this just feels like something great that I can do for myself and maybe for a few others. I don't really want to share the URL here because that's not the reason why I'm making this post. It's to show myself how far I've come and to show others that it is possible to get better after falling down. It might be a long road and it is definitely hard work. But in my opinion, it is worth every drop of sweat. - EDIT: A few of you have asked for the link to the channel because it might be of help to others. If it can help someone who needs it, I'll share it, but keep in mind that the channel is very new and I'm still figuring things out. https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCQHDpWGzxsLSamaERaYKjuQ
item.dc:creator: WeaselBandit

item.dc:creator: Dontforgettofloss

item.title: Hoarder Bedroom cleanup
item.description:
My parents are hoarders. I think it's almost 100% a side effect from being depressed. But they also work long hours and things just kind of... get bad and they don't have the energy to fix it.
But now my mom is retired and wants to do better. The problem is that the mess is too big. She gets anxiety thinking about cleaning it.
And I love my parents. I know this isn't going to "fix" them or keep it from happening again. But it's a step.
So much trash packed down into the carpet. Pulling it up makes it seem so much worse!
Growing up with hoarders makes me a minimalist. And fucking good at cleaning big messes. If it's not clean, I get anxious. So step one was separating trash and salvageable stuff into two basic piles.
Note how small the room feels. That will change!
Really starting to see a difference. Clean laundry on the bed and out of the way.
This was smushed trash between the wall and the bed. Took a whole afternoon.
I had this cleaned off, but needed the counter space for salvageable stuff.
I would take pictures of what I got done at the end of the day. I would come home from work and spend an hour or two on it.
2 weeks in, of here and there cleaning. All trash is gone!
DiY jewelry hanger
Moved the big furniture. Helps with making a new start!
Final product! At least for now.
item.dc:creator: Strongestduck
